content: All JSON-facing types use #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] on structs. String enums use lowercase (AgentState), snake_case (MailMessageType, EventType), or kebab-case (ResolutionTier) per the wire format. The 'type' keyword field gets #[serde(rename = "type")] on a renamed Rust field.

name: TS discriminated union to Rust tagged enum
description: TS discriminated unions map to Rust tagged enums with #[serde(tag = "type")] plus variant-level #[serde(rename = "variant-name")]. MulchRecord uses snake_case fields to match mulch JSON wire format.

title: dead_code allow in scaffold phase
rationale: Use #![allow(dead_code)] crate-level in main.rs during scaffold phases so type modules compile clean under clippy -D warnings before they are wired into commands.

description: reqwest default features require OpenSSL (pkg-config + libssl-dev). Ring crate also needs a working C compiler.
resolution: Use rustls-tls backend: reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "rustls-tls"], default-features = false }. Set LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu if cc1 fails with missing shared libs.

content: Use #![allow(dead_code)] at module level for public API modules (logging/mod.rs) that expose functions for future command modules. In binary crates, even pub fns get dead_code warnings if uncalled. Prefer module-level allow over per-item suppression for large utility modules.

Use #![allow(dead_code)] at module level for public API modules (e.g. logging/mod.rs) that expose functions intended for future command modules. In Rust binary crates, even pub functions get dead_code warnings if uncalled anywhere in the binary.

For Phase 0 CLI skeleton, use PassthroughArgs{args: Vec<String>} with #[arg(trailing_var_arg=true, allow_hyphen_values=true)] for commands with unimplemented subcommands. Define proper Args structs for well-known top-level flags. All commands delegate to not_yet_implemented().

Add #![allow(dead_code)] at module level for public API db modules (db/sessions.rs, db/mail.rs, db/events.rs, db/metrics.rs, db/merge_queue.rs, db/connection.rs) during scaffold phase so stores compile clean without dead_code errors under -D warnings

name: mail-message-field-mapping
description: MailMessage field mapping: DB columns from_agent/to_agent map to struct fields from/to. In row mappers and insert methods, use 'from: row.get(1)?' and 'to: row.get(2)?' not 'from_agent'/'to_agent'.

name: impl_sql_enum-macro
description: Use impl_sql_enum! macro in types.rs to implement ToSql/FromSql for all DB-stored enums (AgentState, RunStatus, MailMessageType, MailPriority, EventType, EventLevel, MergeEntryStatus, ResolutionTier). Requires rusqlite::types imports at top of types.rs.

content: Mail command module exposes standalone execute_list/execute_check/execute_read functions; CLI arg structs (MailArgs, MailSubcommand, MailListArgs, MailCheckArgs, MailReadArgs) are defined in main.rs so clap can parse subcommands, while the command logic stays in commands/mail.rs

name: mail-filter-type-string
description: MailFilters.message_type is Option<String> not a typed enum, so it can be pushed directly into SQL format!() without enum conversion. Only append the SQL clause when Some.

content: doctor command uses vec! macro for initial check collection to satisfy clippy::vec_init_then_push
MergeEntryStatus is defined in types.rs and imported via crate::db::merge_queue only re-exports MergeQueue struct. Import MergeEntryStatus directly from crate::types.

description: Auto-resolve merge silently drops struct definitions when branches modify overlapping files. DoctorArgs struct and mod declarations for doctor/mail were dropped.
resolution: Verify all referenced types and module declarations after merges. Check: (1) all types in Commands enum have matching Args structs, (2) all commands::X references in run_command have matching 'pub mod X' in commands/mod.rs.

name: conflict-marker-state-machine
description: Conflict marker parsing uses a line-by-line state machine (enum ConflictState { Normal, InCanonical, InIncoming }) rather than regex. Start: line.starts_with('<<<<<<< '), separator: line == '=======', end: line.starts_with('>>>>>>> '). Emit incoming lines on end marker; record DisplacedHunk only when canonical content is non-empty after trim.

content: Public API command modules in scaffold phase use #![allow(dead_code)] at module level when not yet wired into main.rs dispatch. Same pattern as other command modules (logging, db). Applies to clean.rs, stop.rs, nudge.rs.
clean::execute uses #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] when function genuinely requires >7 boolean flags (matching CleanArgs flags from clap). Prefer named args struct pattern in future.

description: Adding pub mod init; to mod.rs exposed 3 pre-existing clippy errors in init.rs (double_ended_iterator_last x2, type_complexity x1). These were dormant because init.rs was not declared in mod.rs.
resolution: When fixing merge artifacts on mod.rs, check all newly-added module files for pre-existing clippy issues before declaring quality gates passed. Either fix them in scope or expand scope proactively.

content: Use .next_back() instead of .last() on split('/') and similar DoubleEndedIterator chains — clippy::double_ended_iterator_last fires on .last() calls
Use pattern matching with literal in arm (Ok((stdout, _, 0))) instead of guard clause (if code == 0) — clippy::redundant_guards fires on the guard form

description: serde rename_all on enum only renames variant names, NOT field names in struct variants.
resolution: Add explicit #[serde(rename = "fieldName")] to each struct variant field that needs camelCase.

name: stub-runtime-macro
description: stub_runtime! macro reduces boilerplate for N-variant AgentRuntime impls. Expands id/instruction_path/is_headless/build_headless_command/build_interactive_command/deploy_config/detect_ready/build_env for each stub runtime variant.

name: sling-spawn-pattern
description: Headless spawn uses std::process::Command with stdin piped, writes beacon text to stdin, drops stdin to signal EOF, then std::mem::forget(child) to orphan. TMux spawn uses worktree::tmux::create_session then send_keys with 2s sleep. Both return (Option<i64>, String) = (pid, tmux_session_name).

name: file-scope-extraction
description: agents discover: extract_file_scope reads worktree .claude/CLAUDE.md, finds File Scope section between start/end markers, parses markdown list items with backtick paths. Returns empty vec on any error.

name: event-type-display
description: EventLevel/EventType display: use match on enum variants for level_str(), use serde_json::to_value(et).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_str().map(str::to_string)) for snake_case event type strings. EventType is #[serde(rename_all = 'snake_case')].

name: coordinator-foreground-pattern
description: Coordinator start/foreground pattern: grove coordinator start spawns tmux session running 'grove coordinator start --foreground --project <root>'. The --foreground (hidden clap flag) causes the process to run the event loop synchronously. LoopContext owns String paths (not &str) so it can be passed to blocking run().
